# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

class MarshalSessionStore < ActionDispatch::Session::CookieStore
  def initialize(options = nil)
    super(options.merge serializer: [:marshal, Marshal])
  end

  def load_session(request)
    cookies = request.cookies
    if cookies[:session]
      data = Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])
      session_data = Marshal.load(data)
      if session_data.is_a?(Hash) && session_data.has_key?(:session_id) && session_data[:session_id] == request.session_options[:id]
        return session_data
      end
    end
    {}
  end

  def save_session(request, session, options = nil)
    request.cookies[:session] = {
      value: Base64.encode64(Marshal.dump(session)),
      expires: Time.now + session_expiration_time(options),
      path: options[:path],
      domain: options[:domain],
      secure: options[:secure]
    }
  end
end
